top_gaps_json: [{"id": "PRD-034", "severity": "critical", "label": "Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades", "why": "This matters because the plan never defines migration/backfill behavior, so a schema revision could strand or silently drop existing user collections and My Data."}, {"id": "PRD-033", "severity": "important", "label": "Sync libraries/settings consistently and merge duplicates", "why": "This matters because the plan names merge logic, but not duplicate detection or synced-settings consistency, which creates real cross-device drift and duplicate-record risk."}, {"id": "PRD-091", "severity": "important", "label": "Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity", "why": "This matters because the plan resolves real shows, but it never defines a quality gate for taste alignment, surprise, or discovery regressions before they reach users."}, {"id": "PRD-073", "severity": "important", "label": "Retry malformed mention output once, then fallback", "why": "This matters because Ask depends on structured mentions for follow-on navigation, and a parser miss without an Ask-specific fallback can break the mentioned-shows experience."}, {"id": "PRD-084", "severity": "important", "label": "Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations", "why": "This matters because the plan covers concept grounding, but without an explicit surprise-without-betrayal bar the product can devolve into safe, generic recommendations."}]

top_gap_2_label: Merge catalog fields safely and maintain timestamps. Without explicit merge semantics, catalog refreshes and sync operations can overwrite good data with empties or stale values, which creates subtle correctness bugs across detail pages, home, and AI context
top_gap_3_label: Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades. The plan mentions migrations, but it does not spell out how upgrades protect existing collections, so schema evolution remains a credible source of data loss or broken upgrades

top_gap_2_label: Make client cache safe to discard - If cache/local state disposal is undefined, users can lose trust in persistence after a reinstall, storage clear, or browser reset

top_gaps_json: [{"id": "PRD-072", "severity": "critical", "label": "Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract - Without the exact commentary/showList wire contract, the Ask UI cannot reliably render selectable mentioned shows or recover deterministically when parsing fails", "why": ""}, {"id": "PRD-016", "severity": "critical", "label": "Make client cache safe to discard - If cache/local state disposal is undefined, users can lose trust in persistence after a reinstall, storage clear, or browser reset", "why": ""}, {"id": "PRD-006", "severity": "critical", "label": "Keep secrets out of repo and server-only - Missing secret-boundary planning creates a path to leaking elevated credentials or baking unsafe key usage into the client architecture", "why": ""}, {"id": "PRD-015", "severity": "critical", "label": "Keep backend as persisted source of truth - If backend authority is not explicit, optimistic UI and local state can drift into correctness bugs that resets and sync flows will expose quickly", "why": ""}, {"id": "PRD-086", "severity": "critical", "label": "Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces - Shared AI guardrails are the only thing keeping Scoop, Ask, Explore Similar, and Alchemy aligned on spoilers, scope, honesty, and real-show actionability", "why": ""}]
